rulings.Case Title: IN RE THE MATTER OF THE JAIME AREVALO SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The trustees' attorneys request compensation of $6,003 for their legal services. The requested compensation includes $4,392.50 for the services associated with the prior account. The attorneys have already received $2,630 for their services rendered for the prior account, pursuant to the Court's order settling the prior account. The attorneys have charged $7,022.50 in total for the prior account. 2) The trustees request earlier termination of this trust under Probate Code sections 15202 and 15408 based on its low value and anticipated exhaustion of the trust assets. Although the order on the prior account required this account to cover a two-year period ending on 06/30/2021, the trustees closed this account as of 07/31/2020 with an ending cash balance of $13,867.61. The trustees request a reserve of $12,000 and authority to pay additional fees and costs to their attorneys and other closing expenses without further court approval. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SIGRID ANITA GERDA BORCHERT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Inheritance Rights
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The petition requests the Court's determination that distribution should be made directly to the two successors of the sole heir who survived the decedent but died before final distribution. The petitioner filed a certificate of inheritance issued by the German District Court purporting to identify the two successors of the post-deceased heir domiciled in Germany. The petition alleges that no personal representative is appointed in Germany. Probate Code section 11801(a) provides in relevant part that "the share in a decedent's estate of a beneficiary who survives the decedent but who dies before distribution shall be distributed under this chapter with the same effect as though the distribution were made to the beneficiary while living." Section 11802(a) provides that: "If a beneficiary satisfies the requirement of Section 11801, the beneficiary's share in the decedent's estate shall be distributed as follows: (a) Except as otherwise provided in this section, distribution shall be made to the personal representative of the estate of the beneficiary for the purpose of administration in the estate of the beneficiary. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of All Claims Or Issues
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. The Court DENIES Petitioner Windy Arden's motion to strike statements by Marie Phelps and Movants' Katherine Phelps and Kendall Phelps entire Request for Judicial Notice. The Court SUSTAINS Petitioner's objection to the declaration of Hina Le Calvic submitted in the Request for Judicial Notice, insofar as it appears to be an expert opinion on the relevant law in Tahiti in 2004, and not merely a statement of "The law of an organization of nations and of foreign nations and public entities in foreign nations." (Evid. Code section 452(f).) The Court otherwise GRANTS Movants' request for judicial notice. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) The Court GRANTS Petitioner's request for judicial notice. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Movants seek summary judgment of Petitioner's three pending petitions, or in the alternative, summary adjudication of the issues of Petitioner's alleged legal marriage to Decedent, her alleged legal domestic partnership with Decedent, her alleged status as a putative spouse. The Court DENIES Movants' motion for summary judgment on all Claims, Petitions, Amended Petitions or Amendments. The Court DENIES Movants' motion for summary adjudication in the alternative. Under CCP section 437c(c), the motion for summary judgment shall be granted if all the papers submitted show that there is no triable issue as to any material fact and that the moving party is entitled to a judgment as a matter of law. In determining if the papers show that there is no triable issue as to any material fact, the court shall consider all of the evidence set forth in the papers, except the evidence to which objections have been made and sustained by the court, and all inferences reasonably deducible from the evidence, except summary judgment shall not be granted by the court based on inferences reasonably deducible from the evidence if contradicted by other inferences or evidence that raise a triable issue as to any material fact. Under section 437c(f)(1), a party may move for summary adjudication as to one or more causes of action within an action, one or more affirmative defenses, one or more claims for damages, or one or more issues of duty, if the party contends that the cause of action has no merit, that there is no affirmative defense to the cause of action, that there is no merit to an affirmative defense as to any cause of action, that there is no merit to a claim for damages, as specified in Section 3294 of the Civil Code, or that one or more defendants either owed or did not owe a duty to the plaintiff or plaintiffs. A motion for summary adjudication shall be granted only if it completely disposes of a cause of action, an affirmative defense, a claim for damages, or an issue of duty. In deciding a motion for summary judgment or summary adjudication, the court must refrain from weighing the evidence, assessing the credibility of witnesses, or making any determination of fact. (Weiss v People ex rel Dep't of Transp. (2020) 9 Cal.5th 840, 864; Sandell v Taylor-Listug, Inc. (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 297, 319.) A judge's function at summary judgment is only to decide if disputed issues of material fact make a trial necessary; the judge does not weigh the evidence or assess credibility. (Schmidt v Superior Court (2020) 44 Cal.App.5th 570, 583.) [Part 1 of 2]

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of All Claims Or Issues (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] "A defendant moving for summary judgment is entitled to summary judgment if he or she either conclusively negates an element of the plaintiff's cause of action, or shows that the plaintiff cannot establish at least one element of the cause of action." (Teselle v. McLoughlin (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 156, 176, citing Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 853.) "The party opposing the summary judgment must make an independent showing by a proper declaration or by reference to a deposition or another discovery product that there is sufficient proof of the matters alleged to raise a triable question of fact if the moving party's evidence, standing alone, is sufficient to entitle the party to judgment. [Citations.] To avoid summary judgment, admissible evidence presented to the trial court, not merely claims or theories, must reveal a triable, material factual issue. [Citation.]" (Wiz Technology, Inc. v. Coopers & Lybrand (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 1, 10-11.) Here, much of Movants' initial filing focuses on inconsistencies in the various accounts of marriage offered by Petitioner. (See, e.g. MPA at p. 13 (petitioner has made "repeated and contradictory revisions to the facts, including misstatements.") As noted above, it is not appropriate for the Court to make credibility assessments in deciding a motion for summary judgment. Moreover, although Petitioner's evidentiary showing is relatively thin, Petitioner's August 4, 2021 filing captioned "Statement of Undisputed Facts, In Response and Objection to the Motion for Summary Judgment or, in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication" is signed under penalty of perjury. In that document, Petitioner attests to believing in good faith that she was legally married to Decedent and that he was her husband, that he held himself out as a married man, that she did not know that the San Francisco marriage license was not filed (Id. at 6:2-16, 10:22-25, 11:24-12:2, 13:23). These attestations raise triable issues of material fact as to Petitioner's claims in the pending petitions, and preclude the court from granting Movants' current Motion for Summary Judgment or in the Alternative Motion for Summary Adjudication. Petitioner's request to continue the hearing on this motion is DENIED as moot, given the Court's tentative ruling. Petitioner's request to continue the trial date is DENIED. Petitioner shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, prior to the hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on August 17, 2021. Remote appearance is available, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925#, participant ID 0#, and passcode 722809#. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2]
